title: Nursery Rhyme. XCVII. Proverbs.
content:
[In Suffolk, children are frequently reminded of the decorum due to the Sabbath by the following lines.]

        Yeow mussent sing a' Sunday,
            Becaze it is a sin,
        But yeow may sing a' Monday
            Till Sunday cums agin.
meaning:
Unknown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Nursery Rhyme. XCVII. Proverbs."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
